evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG requester on tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i, SimJTAG, or SimDTM/FESVR debug requester
evidence_slots.operation: issue DMI transactions that may trigger debug entry/reset/system access
evidence_slots.object: debug_req/debug_resp DMI path and debug module outputs debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_master_*, dm_slave_*
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization such as jtag_unlock or equivalent before DMI/JTAG can drive protected debug capability
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals instantiates SimDTM under RISCV_FESVR_SIM and dmi_jtag otherwise. dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock_o as well as dmi_req_o/dmi_req_valid_o. External JTAG pins are directly assigned to internal tck/tms/trst_n/tdi in the non-simulation branch. The provided observation does not include the downstream debug module instance/body showing whether dmi_req_valid/debug_req is gated by jtag_unlock before causing debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, or dm_master transactions.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 256-269 SimDTM; lines 275-297 dmi_jtag; lines 327-332 external JTAG pin assignment; debug signals declared at lines 337-351
evidence_slots.state_condition: testmode_i and we_flag_4 enter dmi_jtag; reset rst_ni/trst_ni observed; jtag_unlock generated but gating of DMI-to-debug-module not shown
evidence_slots.impact: If unauthenticated DMI transactions reach the debug module, an external JTAG/debug requester could halt harts, reset the system, or perform debug-module memory/register accesses.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need debug module instantiation/assignments from dmi_req/debug_req_valid to debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_master_* and whether they are conditioned on jtag_unlock.
inspection_records.notes: Do not elevate to violation based only on top-level DMI/JTAG wiring; the dmi_jtag module may authenticate internally.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG/debug requester influencing jtag_unlock
evidence_slots.object: reglk_mem and reglk_ctrl_o distributed as register-lock controls
evidence_slots.expected_guard: jtag_unlock should itself be authenticated and should not clear/override lock bits except under authorized lifecycle/debug state
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: reglk_wrapper has input jtag_unlock at lines 15/27. reglk_ctrl_o is directly assigned from reglk_mem at line 48. In the sequential block, if ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9), lines 80-84 clear all reglk_mem entries to 'h0. Therefore assertion of jtag_unlock causes all lock-control output bits to zero. Normal writes are only gated by en && we and selected reglk_ctrl bits at lines 86-99.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 275-284 generate jtag_unlock from dmi_jtag; reglk_wrapper.sv lines 25-33, 48, 78-99
evidence_slots.state_condition: When rst_ni is high and rst_9 is low, asserting jtag_unlock makes reset condition true and clears reglk_mem. jtag_unlock authentication source not shown except dmi_jtag hash inputs.
evidence_slots.impact: Clearing reglk_ctrl_o can unlock downstream AES/HMAC/SHA/accounting/register-lock protections, permitting later MMIO writes/reads that policy intended to block.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need dmi_jtag internals to determine strength of jtag_unlock authentication; however the use-site behavior that jtag_unlock clears all locks is evidenced.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: reglk_wrapper resets all register-lock memory whenever jtag_unlock is asserted, so JTAG unlock can clear downstream lock controls rather than only authorizing a narrowly scoped debug action.
inspection_records.notes: This is a use-site permission concern even if jtag_unlock is authenticated: the operation is broad lock clear.

evidence_slots.subject: software or bus master accessing AES MMIO through NoC/AXI-lite bridge
evidence_slots.object: AES start/control, plaintext/block, key registers and state/control registers in aes0/aes1/aes2 wrappers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes to AES key/config/start registers blocked when corresponding reglk_ctrl_i bits indicate locked; all accesses also mediated by acct_ctrl_i
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: All AES wrappers derive en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i after axi_lite_interface. aes0 write block updates start, p_c, key arrays only under en && we and uses reglk_ctrl_i bits in each assignment (e.g. start at line 99, p_c lines 101-107, key writes continue after line 109 in truncated content). aes1 write block lines 241-267 gates control/config/block/key updates using reglk_ctrl_i bits. aes2 similarly uses en && we and reglk_ctrl_i bits for start/p_c/key updates at lines 99-110 and following truncated key writes. Top-level passes per-peripheral acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i] and reglk slices to AES wrappers at aes0 lines 1194-1196; analogous connections are observed by search for aes1/aes2.
evidence_slots.path: aes0_wrapper.sv lines 62-79, 82-109; aes1_wrapper.sv lines 241-267; aes2_wrapper.sv lines 63-79, 84-110; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1187-1199
evidence_slots.state_condition: reglk_ctrl_i locked/unlocked; acct_ctrl_i allow/deny; debug_mode_i affects key material into core but not write enable; per-block resets clear local state
evidence_slots.impact: The observed guards prevent unauthorized or locked MMIO writes from changing AES keys/control when acct_ctrl_i denies or reglk bits are set.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Some aes0/aes2 key-write lines were truncated, but visible pattern and aes1 complete block show lock gating; no contrary write path was provided.
inspection_records.notes: No candidate write-lock violation on provided evidence.

evidence_slots.subject: software or bus master reading AES MMIO through ariane_axi/noc_axilite_bridge
evidence_slots.object: AES key material key0/key1/key2/key_reg* and rdata outputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: secret key/internal state registers should not be readable, or reads should be mediated by strong lock/privilege/debug authorization
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: aes0 read side sets rdata = key0[address[8:3]] before the case at lines 165-168; the case does not override key addresses 5-10, so key0 words can be returned without a visible read lock. aes1 read side returns key_reg0/1/2 when key_we0/key_we1/key_we2 address-decode signals are true, gated only by reglk_ctrl_i[2] at lines 292-294. AES result/status reads are also exposed under reglk bits at lines 280-288. Top-level NoC bridge forwards aes0_axi_resp.r.data to ariane_aes0_buf_noc3_data_o via noc_axilite_bridge lines 1202-1241, and acct_ctrl_i is only acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][1] at line 1195.
evidence_slots.path: aes0_wrapper.sv lines 163-190; aes1_wrapper.sv lines 271-295; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1191-1233
evidence_slots.state_condition: After key write and while acct_ctrl_i permits access; when reglk_ctrl_i read-lock bit is 0 for aes1, and for aes0 key default read lacks explicit lock override in provided lines
evidence_slots.impact: A permitted or misclassified MMIO reader can recover AES secret keys, compromising confidentiality of encrypted data and enabling impersonation/decryption outside the hardware boundary.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: aes2 read mux was not provided; external path shown concretely for aes0 and generally via wrapper AXI outputs.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: AES key registers are readable through MMIO read muxes when read-lock bits are not set, exposing secret key material to any subject allowed by acct_ctrl_i.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate is based on direct key readback behavior; lock bit availability does not by itself satisfy non-readable secret requirement.

evidence_slots.subject: software or bus master accessing HMAC MMIO
evidence_slots.expected_guard: HMAC key and bypass/control configuration writes gated by reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and no debug bypass unless authorized
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: hmac_wrapper derives en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i at line 80. Sequential write logic runs under en && we. Provided write cases show data[14:15] gated by reglk_ctrl_i[3] at lines 160-163; key0[7:0] and ikey/okey hash byte registers gated by reglk_ctrl_i[5] at lines 164-211. key, okey_hash are zeroed to core when debug_mode_i is asserted at lines 54-56, which is not a write bypass.
evidence_slots.state_condition: acct_ctrl_i allow required; reglk_ctrl_i locked prevents updates; debug_mode_i zeroes selected core inputs rather than enabling writes
evidence_slots.impact: The observed guards prevent unauthorized or locked MMIO writes from modifying HMAC keys, derived-key bytes, and data/control registers when access-control and lock bits deny writes.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Beginning of write case for startHash/newMessage/key_hash_bypass was truncated; conclusion relies on visible en/acct gating and provided lock-gated write cases.
inspection_records.notes: No candidate write-lock violation on provided evidence.

evidence_slots.subject: software or bus master reading HMAC MMIO
evidence_slots.object: ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, HMAC hash output, rdata
evidence_slots.expected_guard: HMAC keys, inner/outer key hash bytes, and intermediate state should not be externally readable except intended digest/status under authorization
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: hmac_wrapper read side returns ikey_hash_bytes[7:0] at addresses 34-41 and okey_hash_bytes[7:0] at addresses 42-49, gated only by reglk_ctrl_i[4], lines 278-308. These byte arrays feed the HMAC core as ikey_hash_i/okey_hash_i at lines 321-322 and are part of key-derived state. The wrapper exposes rdata through axi_lite_interface; access is only en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i.
evidence_slots.state_condition: After key or derived hash byte load; acct_ctrl_i permits access; reglk_ctrl_i[4] deasserted; debug_mode_i may zero okey_hash to core but read mux still returns stored okey_hash_bytes
evidence_slots.impact: Exposure of inner/outer key-hash bytes can leak HMAC key-derived secrets and may enable forgery or offline key-recovery attacks depending on HMAC construction and system use.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Earlier read cases for raw key0 were not included; candidate established on derived key bytes alone.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: HMAC derived key material is readable through the MMIO read mux when reglk_ctrl_i[4] is not set.
inspection_records.notes: Digest/hash result reads may be intended; derived-key byte readback is the sensitive behavior.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO requester, reset control path, and JTAG unlock path
evidence_slots.expected_guard: lock bits should have safe reset defaults, become effective downstream, and be sticky or only cleared by authorized reset/lifecycle/debug unlock
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: reglk_ctrl_o is a concatenation of reglk_mem at line 48. The sequential block clears every reglk_mem entry to 'h0 when ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9) at lines 80-84. Thus global reset, jtag_unlock, or rst_9 clears all locks to zero. Normal writes can update reglk_mem when en && we, subject to selected current reglk_ctrl bits at lines 86-99. There is no evidence of write-once OR-only sticky semantics; assignments write wdata directly.
evidence_slots.path: reglk_wrapper.sv lines 48, 78-99; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1814-1819 connect reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl and acct guard
evidence_slots.state_condition: rst_ni low, rst_9 asserted, or jtag_unlock asserted; locked state followed by reset/unlock; en && we writes while acct_ctrl_i permits
evidence_slots.impact: An actor able to trigger the reset/unlock path can clear protections to unlocked defaults, reopening writes/reads on protected AES/HMAC/SHA/accounting registers.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need reset-controller source for who can assert rst_9 and dmi_jtag internals for jtag_unlock authorization.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Register-lock state is not sticky across jtag_unlock or rst_9 and resets to all-zero unlocked state.
inspection_records.notes: The observed semantics are clear/unlocked rather than sticky-safe.

evidence_slots.subject: software or bus master accessing accounting/access-control MMIO, and we_flag_0 input source
evidence_slots.expected_guard: updates to acc_ctrl_o should require authorization and/or lock; once configured/locked, unauthorized writes should not change or force permissions
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: acct_wrapper derives en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i at line 73 and writes acct_mem under en && we with reglk_ctrl bit guards at lines 87-104. However reset condition ~(rst_ni && ~rst_6) initializes every acct_mem entry to 32'hffffffff at lines 81-85. acc_ctrl_o is assigned from acct_mem with acct_mem[0] OR {8{we_flag}} at line 49, so we_flag can force the low policy bits high regardless of stored acct_mem. Top-level passes acc_ctrl to acc_ctrl_c and uses acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][j] to authorize peripherals, lines 216-222 and multiple wrapper connections including acct at lines 1728-1731.
evidence_slots.state_condition: reset defaults; lock state via reglk_ctrl_i; acct_ctrl_i allow; we_flag asserted; rst_6 asserted
evidence_slots.impact: Default-all-allow or forced-high policy bits can grant MMIO access to protected peripherals despite intended accounting policy, enabling unauthorized reads/writes to crypto/control blocks.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need source/authorization of we_flag_0 and full acct_mem address map; visible output expression proves force-open behavior for low policy bits.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Access-control policy registers reset to all allow and include an external we_flag OR into acc_ctrl_o, allowing policy to be forced open outside the stored policy bits.
inspection_records.notes: Policy may intentionally fail-open, but that conflicts with stated expected guard for protected peripherals.

evidence_slots.subject: NoC/AXI bus master reaching peripheral wrappers
evidence_slots.operation: route read/write request to protected peripheral
evidence_slots.object: NoC/AXI routes to AES/HMAC/SHA/reglk/acct/DMA and other protected wrappers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: requests to protected peripherals mediated by acc_ctrl/privilege/address-range checks
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level derives acc_ctrl_c indexed by priv_lvl_i at lines 216-222. Peripheral wrappers receive acct_ctrl_i from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral_index], for example AES0 at line 1195, ACCT at line 1729, REGLK at line 1819, DMA at line 1906, and search results show similar connections for other wrappers. Each inspected wrapper gates its local AXI-lite enable with acct_ctrl_i (AES0 line 78, HMAC line 80, SHA line 74, ACCT line 73, REGLK line 72). NoC bridges connect per-peripheral NoC ports to wrapper AXI requests/responses, e.g. AES0 bridge lines 1202-1233 and ACCT bridge lines 1737-1772.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 216-222, 1191-1233, 1725-1772, 1814-1829, 1898-1906; wrapper en gating lines cited
evidence_slots.state_condition: acc_ctrl allow/deny; priv_lvl_i; valid/ready handshakes; per-wrapper acct_ctrl_i
evidence_slots.impact: When acc_ctrl_c denies the indexed privilege/peripheral combination, local wrapper en is deasserted and MMIO register effects/read data are blocked by the wrapper-level guard.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Top-level address decoding is per dedicated NoC port rather than fully inspected shared decode; no contrary unmediated route to inspected wrappers was provided.
inspection_records.notes: Caveat: policy contents/reset are problematic under PERM-ACCT-POLICY-008, but route use of acct_ctrl appears uniform in inspected wrappers.

evidence_slots.subject: software/bus requester controlling reset peripheral or reset registers
evidence_slots.operation: reset/clear protected peripheral or policy state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: software-controlled resets of crypto, lock, accounting, and debug-related blocks require authorization and safe defaults
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level declares rst_1..rst_13 at lines 902-913 and distributes them to wrappers (AES0 rst_1 at lines 1197-1199; ACCT rst_6 at lines 1732-1734; DMA rst_8 at lines 1910-1912; reglk rst_9 per search/connection at 1814). Wrappers reset local state when their rst_* is asserted: AES0 clears start/p_c/state at lines 84-95; HMAC clears control/data/key state in reset region beginning lines 102-110; SHA clears start/data at lines 96-116. Security policy state is unsafe: acct_mem resets to 32'hffffffff at acct_wrapper lines 81-85, and reglk_mem resets to zero/unlocked at reglk_wrapper lines 80-84.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 902-913, 1191-1199, 1725-1734, 1898-1912; reglk_wrapper.sv lines 80-84; acct_wrapper.sv lines 81-85; sha256_wrapper.sv lines 96-116; aes0_wrapper.sv lines 84-95
evidence_slots.state_condition: peripheral reset asserted while global rst_ni high; locked state before reset; post-reset defaults for reglk/acct
evidence_slots.impact: If a bus requester can assert reset registers without equivalent authorization, it can clear locks and restore permissive access policy, then access protected crypto/control registers.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The source and authorization checks for rst_* generation were not provided; candidate depends on reset peripheral controllability implied by reset NoC port/signals.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Software/bus-visible per-block resets can clear protected lock/access/crypto state to permissive or zeroized defaults; in particular reglk resets to unlocked and acct resets to all-allow.
inspection_records.notes: Even if crypto state zeroization is safe, policy/lock defaults are permissive.

evidence_slots.subject: software or bus master accessing SHA256 MMIO
evidence_slots.expected_guard: SHA control/message registers mediated by acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i; internal hash state not exposed except intended digest/status
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: sha256_wrapper derives en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i at line 74. Write logic runs under en && we and address case beginning lines 117-122 gates startHash with reglk_ctrl_i[1]; data registers are reset safely to 0 at lines 96-116 and subsequent read observations show message data reads gated by reglk_ctrl_i[2]. Read side exposes message data and digest/status: data[13:15] gated by reglk_ctrl_i[2] at lines 198-203, hashValid gated by reglk_ctrl_i[0] at line 205, digest hash words gated by reglk_ctrl_i[4] at lines 207-221. Core receives message block and returns digest at lines 228-238; no internal compression state readback shown.
evidence_slots.state_condition: acct_ctrl_i allow/deny; reglk_ctrl_i read/write lock bits; rst_3 reset; after hash completion
evidence_slots.impact: Observed access controls restrict SHA256 register effects and readback to authorized acct_ctrl and unlocked reglk states; exposed digest/status appears intended output rather than secret internal state.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA engine / DMA NoC port
evidence_slots.operation: DMA read/write to protected MMIO/peripheral region
evidence_slots.object: protected peripheral address ranges and registers reachable from buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i / ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMA-originated requests subject to same acc_ctrl/address-range/PMP-like policy as CPU requests and cannot bypass acc_ctrl/reglk/privilege checks
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level includes a DMA NoC endpoint with dma_wrapper connected to dma_axi_req/resp and acct_ctrl_i = acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8], plus pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i and we_flag_3 at lines 1898-1912. A noc_axilite_bridge maps buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i/valid_i to dma_axi_req AW/W/AR channels and responses to ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o/valid_o at lines 1915-1954. The provided evidence does not show whether dma_wrapper can originate transactions to other protected peripheral NoC/AXI regions or is only a memory-mapped DMA configuration endpoint.
evidence_slots.state_condition: acc_ctrl allow/deny for DMA wrapper; active DMA handshakes; PMP inputs present but wrapper behavior not inspected
evidence_slots.impact: If the DMA wrapper can issue bus transactions to protected MMIO without applying acc_ctrl/PMP/address-range checks, it could bypass CPU privilege and access crypto/control/debug/register-lock regions.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need dma_wrapper internals and fabric connectivity for DMA-initiated outbound requests/address ranges; only the DMA configuration NoC endpoint is evidenced.
inspection_records.notes: Do not mark violation without evidence that DMA can reach protected peripheral regions as a master.
